payload.claim_text: Limited new ridership: existing riders used the fare-free routes more than new route riders
payload.description: 12% of riders were new to the route; existing riders used the route more frequently

records.raw_text: Limited new ridership: existing riders used the fare-free routes more than new route riders.

payload.claim_text: Evaluation compared school months (Sep-May) and summer months (Jun-Aug) pre-pilot vs pilot periods
payload.description: Interim results presented to MTA Board July 2024; full evaluation completed through August 2024

records.raw_text: The MTA presented results from the first nine months to the MTA Board during the NYC Transit Committee in July 2024. The initial assessment examined September to May pre-pilot and during the pilot (school months). The MTA evaluated June through August (summer months) to complete the full evaluation.

payload.claim_text: Pre-pilot, more than one-quarter of new riders used other paid MTA bus and subway service
payload.description: 27% of new riders previously used other MTA bus/subway

records.raw_text: Pre-pilot, more than one-quarter of new riders used other paid MTA bus and subway service.

records.display_name: New and Existing Riders Similar Income

payload.claim_text: New riders and existing riders had similar income profiles
payload.description: 44% of new riders and 46% of existing riders had household income <$28k

records.raw_text: New riders and existing riders had similar income profiles.

payload.description: Bus speeds decreased, dwell times increased, customer journey time performance dipped

payload.claim_text: MTA route selections considered equity, capacity, overlap, and fare evasion
payload.description: Selection criteria for fare-free pilot routes

records.raw_text: MTA's route selections considered: Equity for low income and disadvantaged communities, access to employment and commercial area(s), Existing route capacity, limited crowding conditions, service adequacy, Minimal overlap with other MTA bus services, Fare evasion levels

payload.claim_text: Foregone revenue and related costs amounted to more than $16.5M over the pilot period
payload.description: Includes foregone fare revenue on pilot routes, additional lost revenue, and operational costs

records.raw_text: Foregone revenue and related costs amounted to more than $16.5M over the pilot period

payload.claim_text: Survey of 3,600 Pilot riders and 2,200 pre-Pilot riders
payload.description: RSG survey sample sizes for the pilot evaluation

records.raw_text: Survey of 3,600 Pilot riders and 2,200 pre-Pilot riders

payload.claim_text: 12% of riders were new to the route
payload.description: Existing riders (69% frequency) drove most ridership increases

records.raw_text: While 12% of riders were new to the route, existing riders used the route more frequently and drove ridership increases.

payload.claim_text: Fare evasion on nearby routes was consistent with systemwide rates
payload.description: The rate of fare evasion on nearby routes was consistent with systemwide growth

records.raw_text: Fare evasion on nearby routes was consistent with systemwide rates.

payload.claim_text: New riders were more likely to travel for errands and leisure than for school or work
payload.description: 44% leisure/errands vs 40% school/work for new riders; 30% vs 58% for existing riders

records.raw_text: New riders were more likely to travel for errands and leisure, than for school or work.

description: One-year fare-free bus pilot on five routes (one per borough) mandated by 2023 NY State budget, Sep 2023 - Aug 2024
Part NN of Chapter 56 of the New York Laws of 2023 requires MTA to establish one fare-free bus route in each county of NYC, operating for a minimum of six months and a maximum of one year
Pilot program providing five fare-free bus routes in New York City
Fare Free Bus Pilot with routes selected based on criteria, beginning September 24, 2023.

2023 New York State budget mandated the MTA select and operate one bus route in each borough to be fare free for 6-12 months. Routes: B60 (Brooklyn), Bx18A/B (Bronx), M116 (Manhattan), Q4 (Queens), S46/S96 (Staten Island). Pilot began September 24, 2023. Fare collection resumes on September 1, 2024.
Based on 2023 NYS budget mandating one fare-free bus route per borough for 6-12 months. Results from first 9 months discussed by Jon Kaufman and Chris Pangilinan.
